desc:
This narrow stretch of dry land is hemmed between tall grey cliffs to the east
and an ominously dark lake to the west. The ground is littered with fallen rocks
and boulders, making it difficult to traverse. In the shallows of the lake,
rotting stumps and boughs are all that remain of a once regal grove of holly
trees.
name: A Narrow Stretch of Dry Land

desc:
To the west there is an abrupt drop of several hundred feet. At the foot of the
cliff is a narrow strip of dry land that skirts the fringes of the dark lake
that fills the circular valley to the west. The cliff - and this shelf atop it -
is unnaturally flat. There are cracks or footholds apparent at all in the sheer
rockface. To the east, the mountains begin in earnest, and the peak of Celebdil
Silvertine stands among them like an emperor.
name: The Top of Moria Walls

desc:
This wide shelf runs to the south where it forms the top of a steep cliff that
forms the eastern end of a round valley. The valley is almost complete- ly
filled with an ominous-looking lake - apparently there is some obstruction at
the head of the valley that prevents the water from draining. To the west, one
may proceed along the crest of the ridge that forms the northern edge of the
valley.
name: The Top of Moria Walls

desc:
A number of small stones have been gathered and piled into a small cairn. There
is nothing but rock underfoot so this is certainly not a gravesite - perhaps it
serves some other purpose. At the very least, it indicates that someone besides
the mountain fauna has been here.
name: Cairn on the Mountainside

desc:
To the east one of the first high peaks of the Misty Mountains begins. To the
north and west, the landscape is a monotonous collection of dry hills whose
reddish slopes are littered with boulders. It is possible to ascend a spur of
the mountain to the south - it is a difficult climb of several hundred feet, but
certainly not deadly.
name: Foot of a Mountain

desc:
To the east, the terrain becomes much harsher as the Misty Mountains begin.
Towering over the lower peaks to the east, Celebdil Silvertine reaches for the
sky - its summit gleaming white throughout the year. To the northeast the peak
of cruel Caradhras glowers, and in that direction lies the Redhorn Pass. Farther
west, the dry hills continue - almost a wasteland of barren red rock.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
The hills here are very jagged, making travel extremely difficult. The rocks are
predominantly the colour of rust, and the apparent lack of water makes this a
forbidding landscape indeed. To the east, the high peaks of the Misty Mountains
rear up - dominated by the lofty heights of Caradhras, with Celebdil lying a bit
farther to the south.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
Overlooking a trail or road to the east, whose details cannot be clearly seen
from here, this low hill seems to be almost a sentinel above the road as well as
the plains to the south. Taller mountain peaks rise to the northwest, and some
waving green grasses have grown to the west.
name: The Low Hills

desc:
Guided by your luck you struck a good path. It looks like the remains of an
ancient road, that had once been broad and well planned, from Hollin to the
mountain-pass. The moon, now at the full, rise over the mountains, and casts a
pale light in which the shadows of stone are black. Many of them look to have
been worked by hands, though now they lay tumbled and ruinous in a bleak, barren
land.
name: Path in the Hills

desc:
The way is wet and difficult as pebbles and icy water tumble their way westwards
through the hills. Looking upstream, to the east, the snowy peaks of the Misty
Mountains can be seen rising to the sky far into the horizon. Thick vegetation
flourishes on the northern banks of the river while a faint trail is noticeable
on the sparse southern banks.
name: Narrow River in the Mountain

desc:
Huge piles of splintered and broken rock, hewn apart by the force of it striking
the ground, lie at the bottom of this slope, marking the end result of some
major landslide that occurred in this area. While it is difficult to judge how
long ago this took place, it seems fairly recent, geologically speaking.
name: Among the Mountains

desc:
The first thing you notice is the pile of severed heads which have been
collected into a great pit in the middle of the camp. Dead orc bodies which
those heads belonged to lay scattered all over the ground. The servants of
Sauron are apparently brutal to their friends as well as their enemies. A huge
building rises up to the south, overlooking the smashed burnt huts.
name: Camp on the Hills

desc:
This appears to have been a very impressive camp at one time or another.
Destroyed huts and burnt fences attest to its past vitality. However, it seems
that this area has been pillaged and overrun by the armies of the Dark One.
Surprisingly, there are no indications of battle. Evidently, such chaos is not
rare among the forsaken servants of Melkor.
name: Camp on the Hills

desc:
An encampment covers the land to the west. The Misty Mountains rise to the
north, and hills wander east and south.
name: Hills outside the Camp

desc:
Corpses and skeletons are strewn across the ground. Some are pierced by strange,
small arrows which seem to have killed them without warning. You recognise some
of the skeletons as that of humans and dwarves. Strange, twisted trees torture
the earth with their existence, forming a ring about the area which allows very
little light with which to see by. Gnarled roots infest the ground and you feel
you would not like to touch them!
name: The Hills with Black trees

desc:
Corpses and skeletons are strewn across the ground. Some are pierced by strange,
small arrows which seem to have killed them without warning. You recognise some
of the skeletons as that of humans and dwarves. Strange, twisted trees torture
the earth with their existence, forming a ring about the area which allows very
little light with which to see by. Gnarled roots infest the ground, and you feel
you would not like to touch them!
name: The Hills with Black trees

desc:
You are standing at the base of a beautiful peak in the mountains. A cool and
refreshing wind blows here. You find yourself understanding the dwarven love for
these cold, rocky mountains. To the south however, the land takes on very
ominous undertones.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
No folk dwell in these lands now, but many other creature live here at all time,
especially birds. Yet now all things but you are silent. You can feel it. There
is no sounds for miles about you, and your voices seems to make the ground echo.
You do not understand it.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Climbing ever higher, you stand high atop a range in the Misty Mountains.
Impressive, snow-capped peaks rise up all around you, providing a majestic view
of the surroundings. To the north, the mountains sink down into a range of
hills, dwarfed by the larger peaks. The air here is thin and cold forcing you to
keep moving to stay warm.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Trudging along this huge mountain range you can only take the time to notice the
countless peaks around you. A range of hills lies to the north but you cannot
head that way, as a small river is flowing in a deep canyon just between you and
these friendly hills. The mountains block your way south and east.
name: Narrow River in the Mountains

desc:
You are walking in a plain covered by bushes, at the foothills of the
Hithaeglir. A smooth path has been delineated in a small ravine, partially
hidden beyond the high bushes. To the near south are hills, tall in themselves
but dwarfed by the mountains a bit more south. The dangerous tales you have
heard all seem true as you look upon that huge range. The peaks lie near the
clouds and you can hear the screaming winds that echo through the mountains.
name: Bushy Plains

desc:
You are walking in a trail among the first hills of Hollin. You have reached the
foot of the Hithaeglir proper. Looking south you can see them standing proud and
mighty. You remember the tales of your youth and your grandmother telling you
that these mighty peaks were thrown up in the path of the elves by Morgoth in
order that he might stop them reaching the West.
name: Secret Trail in the Plain

desc:
A pleasant trail runs through the hills here. Although it does not appear to be
used often, it still has not become overgrown. You can continue on the trail to
the east or south.
name: Peaceful Trail

desc:
You feel something ancient and great here. A warm and peaceful feeling fills
your body. You feel that this is perhaps the only place among these hills that
the servants of Sauron have not defiled with their presence.
name: A Lonely Hill

desc:
Little grows on these hills except grass. There are several small bushes trying
to survive but they are not doing so well. Apparently, the cold harsh weather is
too much for them. The sides of the Caradhras are now dark and sullen, and its
head is in grey cloud.
name: The Hills

desc:
The complex is large, extending a fair distance to the east. Some equipment and
debris in the corner seems to indicate that this may be a headquarters for
Orkish captains. However, the room seems to have been used more as a shelter
from the sun than as a base of operations since the room appears very
disorganized and, for the most part, empty.
name: The Huge Wooden Building

desc:
You can see that at one time this building had many rooms. But now, all the
walls have been torn down to create one huge hall. You can see a rotten corpse
hanging from the ceiling. A great deal of debris lies about here and it smells
fairly musty.
name: The Huge Wooden Building

desc:
As the shallow stream to the east enters this wide plain, it spreads out across
the ground and covers the land with slow moving and very shallow water. Some
small plants have taken root here, thriving on the lull in the stream, helping
to hinder its progress further. The water continues to slowly flow to the south
and west.
name: The Flooded Plain

desc:
The place has served as a dumping-ground and graveyard. There are many
half-rotten corpses and bits of trash which you don't want to guess the source
of. The stench is awful!!! The hills stretch to the south and east and the
ominous building looms over you to the north. A small stream flows here, its
water poisoned by the garbage dropped in it.
name: Backyard

desc:
You stand at the back of the ugly, wooden building. The plain continue south,
east and north. A small stream, coming from a marish that you can smell at the
foot of the mountains, flows next the building. It is used obviously used as a
sewer by the inhabitants of the house, and garbage has been thrown into the
clear water, poisoning it.
name: Backyard

desc:
You are walking in a flat prairie, smoothed by a glacier many generations ago.
The grass grows high, watered by several small streams flowing from the high
summits around. You clearly see the great Misty Mountains guarding this land
from the east, south, and north. You also notice some smoke rising from a
strange structure to the west.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
The wooden building looks very quickly (and very poorly) built. But it's too big
to serve any mundane purpose and somehow it makes you shiver in disgust. The
ominous structure makes you want to leave as soon as possible.
name: The East Wall of the Strange Building

desc:
You see some grassy hills surrounding you. Some withering trees are feebly
attempting to grow in this cold, harsh climate. Regiments of black crows are
flying over all the land between the Mountains and the Greyflood, and they have
passed over Hollin. They are not native here; they are "crebain" out of Fangorn
and Dunland. You do not know what they are about: possibly there is some trouble
away south from which they are fleeing; but you think they are spying out the
land. You also glimpse many hawks flying high up in the sky. Hollin is being
watched.
name: The Hills

desc:
This is the last part of flat prairie. The plains end here, cedeing the place to
the barren mountains. A cold wind blows from the east. Several small bushes and
a tree or two are trying to grow here but the climate is not cooperating. Only
the spruces and firs look healthy.
name: Cold Plain

desc:
It is strange how these hills confuse you. They all look very similar.
Essentially there is nothing distinct in any of them. At least you can use the
sun and the stars to figure out which direction you are heading but you
definitely can not tell one hill from another.
name: The Hills

desc:
This path seems seldom used. There is something great and powerful here. It
seems so ancient and beautiful. You surprise yourself by thinking that the
plants adorn the path as if they were honouring it.
name: Peaceful Trail

desc:
You climb to the top of the small hill under which you had been sheltering. It
is crowned with a knot of old and twisted trees, about which lay a broken circle
of boulder-stones. In the midst of this is a good place to light a fire, for
there is no hope that darkness and silence would keep your trail from discovery
by the wild beasts who live here. You are surrounded by high snow-covered peaks.
The cold air and the strong winds here give you good reason to light a fire or
leave.
name: Hollin Hills

desc:
You walk through the northern hollin plains, where Elves and Dwarves once built
a rich kingdom. East you can see great mountain peaks covered with snow. They
look beautiful and noble, lurking above you. The giant mountains seem to tower
into the heavens. Mother nature can be so lovely and at the same time so
threatening.
name: Hollin Hills

desc:
The grass grows high in these plains, watered by a clear stream which flows from
a valley east. It is not easy to walk through the muddy prairie, but it is the
only way to reach the road south.
name: Hollin Hills

desc:
Descending to the east very gradually, these mountains overlook a wide plain in
that direction through which a thin thread of a road travels. Other, taller
mountains stand to the west and south, while they taper and flatten to the
north.
name: Edge of the Hills

desc:
Pools of stagnant water have formed in places here, where the stream passing
through keeps the ground saturated without washing away the swampy sludge that
develops. The water flows slowly to the west, while mountain peaks dominate the
south.
name: The Flooded Plain

desc:
It is strange how these hills confuse you. They all look very similar.
Essentially there is nothing distinct in any of them. At least you can use the
sun and the stars to figure out which direction you are heading but you
definitely can not tell one hill from another.
name: Hollin Plains

desc:
It is strange how these hills confuse you. They all look very similar.
Essentially there is nothing distinct in any of them. At least you can use the
sun and the stars to figure out which direction you are heading but you
definitely can not tell one hill from another. A small trickle of water flows
north, coming from the eastern hills.
name: Hollin Plains

desc:
It is strange how these hills confuse you. They all look very similar.
Essentially there is nothing distinct in any of them. At least you can use the
sun and the stars to figure out which direction you are heading but you
definitely can not tell one hill from another. A small trickle of water flows
west, coming from the boggy plain south.
name: The Hills

desc:
A smelly swamp covers this area. Numerous reeds and lilies grow here and there
are many insects buzzing around. The swamp continues to the west and a small
rivulet exits north through some hills.
name: The Smelly Swamp

desc:
Disgusting, smelly mud covers part of this hill. After studying it a moment, you
come to the conclusion that this area was once a small lake some years ago which
degenerated into this swamp. You must be careful where you step.
name: The Smelly Swamp

desc:
It is strange how these low flat hills confuse you. They all look very similar.
Essentially there is nothing distinct in any of them. At least you can use the
sun and the stars to figure out which direction you are heading but you
definitely can not tell one hill from another.
name: Hollin Plains

desc:
Marking the northeast corner of this particular group of hills, these heights
help to contain water in a shallow pool to the north, affording a ford to a
stream that passes through. The hills continue south and west, out of sight, and
some lower plains separate these heights from others to the east.
name: Corner of the Hills

desc:
You are in a small range of grassy hills. Some small trees and bushes grow here
but the cold climate is not treating them well. The Caradhras rises before you,
tipped with snow like silver, but with sheer naked sides, dull red as if stained
with blood.
name: The Hills

desc:
You wander through these endless mountains, growing weary of the journey. The
Hithaeglir go on in all directions, but here to the north and south rough grey
faces of rock enclose you. In the northern face, a small opening to perhaps a
cave can be seen. The western wall seems to have many crevices in it, the strong
stone shattered by ice and wind. East and south the pass goes on, shadowed by
the high rocks.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
You are walking through some peaceful hills. These hills are almost like a big
valley in the middle of the mountain range. Only a slight breeze blows here and
it is fairly warm.
name: Small Hills

desc:
The wind here is devastating. It is drawing every ounce of heat out of your
body. You knew it would be cold up here but you did not expect this. It is
almost like the mountains do not want you to get out alive. You can travel along
a mountain pass north or west, but a steep slope covered by fallen boulders
blocks your way south.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
The path leaves the cliff and runs into a wide shallow trough at the bottom of a
long hard slope. You have no shelter there from snow, or stones - or anything
else. Westward one can see the luxurious plains of Eregion. A darker strip of
vegetation can be seen through the hills: The Bruinen, flowing to the sea. The
cold wind cruelly takes your thoughts from the beautiful peaks, and you continue
your trek onward.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
Only a few paces from the path, the snow lay many feet deep, higher than the
head of a hobbit; in places it has been scooped and piled by the wind in great
drifts against the cliff. You also notice that you reached a bend of this
mountain pass. You will have to turn around since the cliffs are too steep here
to use.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
You have reached the middle of the Redhorn pass. South there lies only death at
the bottom of a deep canyon. The view here is spectacular though. Many of these
peaks reach into the clouds. You might as well enjoy the view for a while before
turning back on the mountain pass.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
Very little lives in the cold high mountains. There is some short grass, a few
fungi and several beetles are crawling around but that is all you can see. That
is probably a hint that you should not stick around too long. The trail you are
on is very difficult to follow.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
You risk your life climbing in these mountains. The mountain sides that are not
covered with snow are very rocky. You must walk very carefully, or soon you will
be sliding down the mountain side among tons of loose rocks. If you leave the
Redhorn Pass, you may certainly find a bad end at the bottom of a cliff.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
The path travels south and west here up the side of the mountain. To the north,
the mountain side is too steep for climbing, and would be perilous to attempt to
ascend. The loose rocks strewn about have a slight reddish color in striking
contrast to the blindly white snow that blankets the ground most of the year.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
A strange path begins here. Grass is trampled as if a huge animal had been
pacing the earth here. The Misty Mountains rise north and east from here.
name: A Little Trail through the Hills

desc:
You move along the base of a beautiful and majestic peak in the range. The wind
is warmer here than in some of the higher altitudes, providing a soothing balm
instead of draining you of heat.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
You are standing at the base of a beautiful peak in the mountains. A cool and
refreshing wind blows here. Despite the narrow paths, the precipitous drop, and
the fierce weather you now understand the dwarven love for these mountains.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
You are standing at the base of a beautiful peak in the mountain. It stands high
in the south. You think for a bit and seem to recognise it from a tale that you
heard. Maybe this is the legendary Caradhras.
name: Before the Great Peak

desc:
At this juncture deep within the Misty Mountains, you see continuing peaks to
the east, south, and west. Tall hills lie to the north, in contrast to the
massive jutting rocks everywhere else. Despite the heights, you could explore
among any of the peaks around here.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Moving around the boulders and rocks, you notice some hardy plantlife growing
from what seems to be solid rock. Though an encouraging sight, being surrounded
on all sounds by the Misty Mountains leaves no doubt that it will be a long time
before viewing verdant forests or golden plains.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Despite the forbidding rocky terrain, you notice some short and dry grasses
growing amongst the stones. Listening carefully, you hear the sounds of the
insects and beetles that are the main inhabitants in the frigid, dry air.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
The endless vista of the towering Misty Mountains fills your view. Only to the
north is there a break, a line of tall hills that plunge into the mountain
range. The air is cold and dry, forcing you to stop often to catch your breath.
Pausing a second, you consider your alternatives.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
On this winding ledge through the mountains, you stand near the top of this
mountain. Gasping for air, you pause and see more of the same peaks everywhere
but to the north. A range of hills lies to the north but they are much too far
away for you to get to from here.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Walking through the endless range of mountains, the thought of returning to a
home in an idyllic valley fights with the thirst for adventure. The sight of
some stubborn plants does little to assuage the growing despair in your heart.
An echoing howl immediately shakes you from your contemplations and you grip
your weapon tightly.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Short and dry grass is trying to make a living here. You can see some of the few
animals that live in these lifeless mountains. There are some insects and
beetles, nothing more. The Misty Mountains now surround you.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Surrounding you on three sides, the Misty Mountains stand tall in ridges to the
east, south, and west. Some tall and rocky hills are to the north, offering a
possible way down from these eerie heights. To the west, it might be possible to
find a path among the rocky peaks.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
In these cold airless mountains only small life can be found. You see some short
grasses, some moss and some small insects and beetles. You are surprised that
even that much can live here. The Misty Mountains surround you on all sides.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
A small amount of life can be found here, Some short grass grows and there are a
few insects. At this height you can not expect much else. You are surrounded by
the Misty Mountains.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Scanning the horizon, you see an endless vista of rocky peaks. However, to the
south, you do spot a range of hills, but you see no way of getting to them from
here. You brace yourself among the loose rocks and prepare to continue.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Hiking along an east-west path through the Misties, you keep your eyes on the
range of hills that lie to the south. Your tired legs would appreciate a break
from the steep slopes, but there is no way climb down at this point. Taking a
deep breath of the thin, cold air, you press onward.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
The mountains seem to climb only higher and higher from here. It is difficult
and treacherous climbing here and you are quickly exhausted. You see a great
peak of the mountains against the horizon to the south.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
The mountains seem to climb only higher and higher east and north from here. A
huge peak rises in the mountains to the south. To reach it you will have to
climb down the narrow ravine which separates the two summits, and then up the
rocky slope on which a path can be observed. Just to the west are rolling hills
but it is far too dangerous to climb to them from here.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
A cool spring is here surrounded by big rocks and short grass. Many strange
footprints mark the ground near this cool fountain which seems to be one of the
few sources of water in these cold hills.
name: A Spring at the Hills

desc:
This narrow trail twists and turns through the hills. Numerous large tracks are
in the surface here. They definitely imply something bigger than a human but you
are not sure what made them. It is best to stay cautious as you continue.
name: A Little Trail through the Hills

desc:
This little trail winds through the hills. Something uses this trail often but
you are not sure what it is. The tracks you see here are quite large and are
probably made by something that would be dangerous to run into. The trail
continues north and east.
name: A Little Trail through the Hills

desc:
You are walking through some small, friendly hills. These hills are like a big
hole in the middle of the mountains and higher hills. The wind is not blowing
here and the air is warm.
name: Small Hills

desc:
This little trail winds through the hills. It seems often used, but by whom? The
prints that mark the trail seem too great to have been made by a man. The Misty
Mountains rise to the north, east and south.
name: A Little Trail through the Hills

desc:
This little trail winds through the hills. It is definitely used often but at
this point you are not sure what creature or creatures use it. You should
probably be on guard as you move along here. The path continues south and west.
name: A Little Trail through the Hills

desc:
This ravine has been created by a narrow stream which comes from the snowy peaks
to the east. It separates the two high mountain ranges, north and south of you.
Two unclimbable waterfalls block the way east and west. Having grown a bit
accustomed to the stunning view around you, the subtle differences become more
apparent. To the south, the mountains are not too steep, making it possible to
enter them from this point.
name: Narrow Ravine

desc:
Some short grass and a little moss is trying to grow here and you can see a few
beetles crawling around. That seems to be the extent of the life around here.
You are now walking on the north side of a steep rocky slope, beneath the
RedHorn Pass. From this site you have an exceptional view over the plain in the
north-west, formerly known as Rhudaur.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
You are walking in the last hills this side of the mountains. There are much
higher than the surrounding high hills, and are covered by rocks and sand which
fell from the steep mountain slope. The Misty Mountains dominate your eastern
horizon but there is no way to enter them from here.
name: Sandy Hills

desc:
You are walking through some small pleasant hills. They are a nice break from
the higher hills and mountains surrounding this area. Little wind blows here and
the air is relatively warm.
name: Small Hills

desc:
The trail is winding up along the steep side of Caradhras, taking often the
sudden turns but still holding its general course north-south. West of you, the
rock wall goes down for several hundred feet. A magnificent view over the lower
Misty Mountains rolls open before your eyes.
name: A Trail at the Slope

desc:
Here the trail bends west still rises to the north, Looking down over the edge
to the west makes you dizzy and reminds you to proceed with extra caution. The
ever blowing chilly wind seems to cut through your clothes and flesh. Mighty
Redhorn is rising high to the east of here.
name: Mountain Trail

desc:
The trail bends yet again, continuing its steady rise to the north. Examining
the ground, you notice few animal tracks, too old though to say anything more
precise. From here it is also possible to leave the track and descend into the
lower foothills of the Redhorn. Caradhras is silently towering above you to the
east of here.
name: Mountain Trail

desc:
The narrow mountain path bends here, descending sharply to the lower mountains
to the west and continuing its steady rise towards the north. East of you,
Caradhras is rising high into the sky, Its mountainside being too steep to
climb. An icy wind is lashing through your clothes, making it difficult to walk.
name: Mountain Trail

desc:
A hot, steaming spring is giving life to this small, well hidden valley between
the surrounding mountains. The rocky ground is covered by low grass and a few
sparse bushes are around. The valley mouth to the west leads to the Misty
Mountains. The air is wet here and the rock walls are offering protection from
the icy wind outside.
name: A Valley

desc:
Dull and cold mountains surround you from all sides. The ground is bare and no
vegetation can be seen anywhere. An icy wind is making it even harder to retain
your balance while climbing between the rocks and you have to be extra careful,
as there is not much around to offer any support should you fail. A narrow split
in the mountains leads to a small valley to the east.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
The mountains seem to climb ever higher to the east, culminating with the
Redhorn at the summit. To the west, a majestic view opens over the lower peaks
of the Misty Mountains, with the green Hollin as a hinterland.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Here the Misty Mountains stretching out to the west slowly melt into the lower
foothills of the Caradhras to the east. A narrow trail is running along the
mountain ridge right above you, the gound has a downward slope to the west.
name: Foothills of Caradhras

desc:
The trail takes a sharp turn over the corner of the rock, bending southeast,
while beginning to rise steeply in that direction. As the trail is winding
higher, it becomes more exposed to the wind making it sound like a good idea to
pay more attention to every step you take. About the only plants you can see
here are the low bushes growing in the natural rock niches.
name: A Narrow Track at Mountain Ridge

desc:
The trail is running steeply downhill to the west, barely noticeable among the
fallen rocks and boulders. However, after rising steeply east of here it seems
to become more distinct and continue its path towards the north.
name: Trail up to the Mountain

desc:
The trail winds along the mountain-side, now and then running dangerously close
to the edge of a ravine to the west. Southwards, the ground seems to slope
gradually lower. North-east of here, the snowy peak of Caradhras is rising the
the sky.
name: A Narrow Track at the Mountain Ridge

desc:
Narrow trail leaves the Redhorn Pass here, running further to the north. It is
not very often used by its appearance and you are barely able to follow its
course between the rocks. Sheer rock walls block the way both to the east and
west. There is no vegetation around as far as your eyes can see.
name: A Narrow Mountain Trail

desc:
South and east the Redhorn Pass bends as it continues up the mountain's heights,
steadily gaining altitude. Occasionally an animal dropping may be seen on the
ground, sometimes fresh and sometimes many days old. The path winds its way
amidst the many large rocks and boulders scattered about.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
The path twists through a crevice in the mountain side, to the north and south
it rises out of the natural trench. The walls, east and west, are jagged and are
too treacherous to climb. The snow piles up here if there is any around at all,
a foreboding ill of the heights above.
name: Crevice in the Pass

desc:
Here and there a stunted fir clutches half-heartedly at the sloping ground, as
the Pass bends south and west. Their branches hang sadly, suggesting the sun is
seldom seen. The ground is covered with loose rock making footholds unsteady,
forcing travelers to keep their eyes on the ground and not the view.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
The rocks underfoot begin to roll and slide. You reach out for something to
grab. A tree branch passes by, just out of reach. You begin to pick up speed as
you roll down this steep crevice. Head over heels you tumble, scraping knees and
hands. Alas, you come to rest at the bottom of the crevice. Buried under a small
mountain of boulders. The wind howls, crying out as the mighty Redhorn claims
another life. May the gods be kind to your soul.
name: Bottom of a Crevice

desc:
The path curves northward and eastward around a rough stone outcropping in the
mountain side. The lee of the small overhang provides a small degree of respite
from the snow which plagues this evil mountain the elves name Caradhras. The
evil seems to grow more palpable the higher up the mountain a traveler gets.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
An outcropping can be seen looking westward, while the path heading upward heads
south. The Pass is steeper here than below, and appears to continue the trend
farther up. Only a short distance away the trail bends east out of sight, and
the mountain races upward in the northeast direction here.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
Descending to the north, the path leaves this icy plateau. The ground is in a
constant state of being frozen, with almost no life visible. A few of the rocks
bear small patches of lichen and moss. Heading into the centre of the ice plain,
the trail goes east where it can be seen for a relatively long stretch.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
The path winds down along the slopes of the mountain, here leading north a short
distance before it can be seen to twist again. Climbing west up the path it
rises determinedly. A pause here allows a traveller a stunning view. The
southern end of the great Misty Mountain range lies over the valley between
Caradhras and its nearest southern neighbour.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
The trail twists east and south hugging the mountain side. This seems to be at
the top of the would-be treeline; a few haggard firs dot the view, and south
there is naught of vegetation but low shrubs and harsh grass. Looking downslope
the flora grows in health and strength, as though the ground is poisoned in the
heights.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
Massive boulders lie about, some in somewhat unusual positions, almost as if
they had fallen from higher up the mountain. The path climbs westward and the
descent southward seems to be less steep. Scratches and marks in the snow and
ground seem to be left by some type of animal, and they seem to lead north if
anywhere.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
The slope of the trail is hardly mild, yet, compared to the mountain side west
and south, it could be said to be. Rising northward, more of the huge rocks can
be seen farther up the path. There do not seem to be any of them to the east
where the path goes down, however they are replaced with an increasing abundancy
of plant life. Many trees root in the mountain side, and in the short summer
wildflowers are everywhere.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
It becomes difficult to know exactly where the path lies in the unchanging ice
which covers the landscape during most of the year. In the few months of summer
the ground is covered in gravelly rock shards and pebbles that the ice grinds
from the earth as it freezes and melts. The plains drop away abruptly to the
south, and with the ice and bits of stone it is clearly impossible to attempt to
go there. The northern side of the plateau is equally as treacherous and
unattainable.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
The long narrow stretch of ground here extends westward in a gradual descent,
yet northward the path climbs much more quickly. The plains fall away all at
once in all other directions as it comes to a head and curves away. Uncountable
numbers of tiny rock bits and stones randomly cover the terrain, cracked from
the land by the forces of the seasons.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
A huge gorge runs from the north to the east down the side of the mountain and
forces the Pass up and around, here being westward and to the south. A brave
traveller might look down a way into the depths of the tear in the earth, but
only a fool would do so in the winter. Be it courage or stupidity, the view is
striking as the exposed red rock descends into blackness. A jagged, towering
crag protrudes from the western cliff wall.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
The gorge passes eastward from the north and away to the southeast, while the
Pass rises up sharply northward paralleling it. Descending east, the path runs
along the west lip of a narrow canyon. Attempting to follow this scant trail in
the winter, with mighty Caradhras at his peak in snowy power would be more than
foolish, and only the greatest of luck would save such a poor soul.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
Higher and higher the trail ascends into the mountain from below. The gorge
narrows to the east where a sturdy stone bridge passes over it while the slope
to the north-west remains much too steep to climb. The red rock all around you
is what obviously gives this area its mortal name: Redhorn.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
Wise travelers would only cross this bridge in the few months of summer, when it
is not iced over and covered with snow. The wind whips through the canyon neck
below, down out of the mountain. It runs a short distance farther north, then is
lost to the mountain. South it opens up and the path can be seen to wind along
its side. While the bridge has stood here for countless years, and shows no sign
of weakening, it still is treacherous footing with bad weather.
name: Bridge in the Pass

desc:
The slope eastward is unpassable, yet the north might be attainable to one with
enough prowess, as it offers an uneven surface where one might, if they were
deft, find foot- and hand-holds. There is a gorge to be seen in the distance to
the west, and the path leads in that direction to a heavy bridge spanning it.
This must be the highest point in the Redhorn Pass, for in both directions the
path descends, to the south quite steeply.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
The path climbs steeply uphill to the north, and almost as sharply to the south.
Beyond the western edge of the trail, the ground slopes very sharply down to the
lip of a deep gorge. If on a rare peaceful day this high up in the Redhorn
Mountain, one of the small rocks which lie strewn about happens to get kicked
down over the edge, the sound of its falling fades before any distinctive noise
of hitting the bottom.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
Curving eastward over a ridge trailing down the mountain, the path rises only
for a moment before resuming its descent. Ice and snow, once in a while with the
marks of an animal passing, lie over the red stone the majority of the seasons.
Down the near-drop southwest, a thin canyon winds down the mountain side.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
Many rocks, obviously fallen from the higher mountains, are scattered about and
around the small trail here. This is much narrower and less even than the
Redhorn Pass, which leaves just south. There is no vegetation around, just the
rock walls rising high to the west and north. To the east you notice a sheer
rock face.
name: Mountain Trail

desc:
You are standing in an ample natural cavern which continues further into the
darkness to the north. A solid slab of stone blocks the way to the west. The
ground here has been flattened from the continuous trod of orkish feet.
name: A Cave Entrance

desc:
The tunnel coming in from the east opens to a spacious guardroom with cold rock
walls. A thick layer of soil is on the ground as well as some broken pieces of
furniture. There are few bunks by the walls.
name: A Guardroom

desc:
The tunnel has been carved through the solid red rock here, slightly sloping
upwards to the west. The air is stagnant and no light can be seen anywhere. The
ground is still uneven, although smoothed by many orkish feet. Occasional
distant shouts break the silence now and then.
name: Tunnel under the Mountain

desc:
The red stone walls of the cavern rise high into the darkness here, up to the
unseen ceiling high above. The dank tunnels leaving east and north seem to be
natural, whereas the one going west is clearly of orkish origin.
name: Under the Mountain

desc:
The tunnel sharply bends to the north here, becoming considerably lower in that
direction. To the west, a much higher cavehall can be seen through the darkness.
From the narrow opening in the east wall, a strong smell of animals eminates,
making the already foul air even worse.
name: Tunnel Bend

desc:
The tunnel ceiling beams are so low that you have to bend your head to be able
to pass through. To the south, the tunnel turns right, and in the opposite
direction it seems to become even lower and narrower. There is a heavy oaken
door with a small watching hole in the east wall.
name: A Low Tunnel

desc:
The low, greystone tunnel you are standing in continues to the south where it
seems to widen slightly; to the north it opens to a dark chamber. East of here a
natural burrow leaves the passage.
name: A Low Tunnel

desc:
You have reached a dark cave, filled with different devices of the precise
purpose of which you are unsure. There are a few benches around and a pair of
chains fastened to the rock wall. The walls and the floor are bloodstained and
the very air itself seems to be tainted by evilness. A wooden grid covers a
large pit in the centre of the room.
name: Torture Chamber

desc:
A small niche leaves the main cave here. It has the same red stone walls and
trampled ground. Two torches are attached to the wall, giving off only a little
light. There is an open furnace in the east wall, blasts of hot air are coming
from there. A heavy iron anvil stands by the south wall.
name: A Niche

desc:
The tunnel, with its red rock walls, is coming from the south. As it bends west
it becomes obviously higher and although no doubt natural, it also bears quite a
few marks of recent expanding. To the west, some orkish voices can be heard.
name: Tunnel Bend

desc:
The tunnel has been hewn into the red rock, bending to the west and north. There
are few loose stones on the ground, obviously left from a recent expanding of
the passage. The walls bear the marks of the metal hammers but are still rather
smooth and even.
name: Tunnel Bend

desc:
Here the tunnel is running straight east-west through the mountain. It is
clearly not natural, walls and the floor bearing the clear marks of orkish tools
used in carving it through the red rock. The air stands still and the silence
seems to become even denser to the west, while from the east some distant orkish
voices can be heard.
name: Tunnel into the Mountain

desc:
The tunnel divides into three separate branches here. The main passage runs east
to west while another small cavern lies to the north, its entrance carved
carefully from the red rocks of the mountain. The tunnel floor is even and
smooth here, looking almost polished.
name: Tunnel Junction

desc:
The tunnel comes to an abrupt end here, stopping at the piles of loose rocks and
debris along the west wall. Obviously the expanding of the caves has been halted
here for the time being. The only exit is back to the east.
name: Tunnel End

desc:
You are standing in a cavern with a perfectly even floor. The stone walls are of
the red rock of Caradhras, but carefully smoothed. To the east there is a
doorway, covered with a silvery grey warghide. A single torch has been attached
to the wall, illuminating the room with a low yellow flame.
name: A Natural Cavern

desc:
You have entered a surprisingly comfortable room. Although hewn into the red
rock of Caradhras, the walls are smooth and the ground is even, covered with a
carpet. The cosy bed is by the east wall and a golden plaque with ancient runes
has been attached to the wall right above it. The heavy wooden shelf with
different books and few parchment scrolls is standing next to the bed. There is
a low black door in the north wall. A half-burned candle is standing atop of the
heavy oaken table, dimly illuminating the room.
name: A Dimly Lit Cavern

desc:
Here the passage bends yet again. Running through the red rock mountain base, it
twists to the north and east, rising slightly in that direction. The deadly
silence surrounds you and no light can be seen anywhere.
name: A Twisting Tunnel

desc:
The narrow tunnel is coming from the south and bends sharply to the west. On the
eastern wall there is an opening, leading to the smaller cave. Litter and
smaller rocks cover the ground everywhere.
name: A Twisting Tunnel

desc:
The stone tunnel runs north-south here, apparently widening to the south. Some
flickering lights can also be seen in that direction. It has slightly descending
slope towards north and the passage can be seen twisting left shortly there.
name: A Narrow Tunnel

desc:
Long shadows cast from a dimly lit cavern to the east illuminate the red stone
typical to Caradhras. This worn tunnel slopes gently to the north and continues
a winding path southwards.
name: A Tunnel Intersection

desc:
A small, totally isolated room has been carved into the solid mountain, no voice
from outside can reach here. The austere stone walls and low ceiling further
emphasizes the isolation, and it is obvious that this room is not visited very
often. The stacks of books by the walls are covered by a thin layer of dust. A
low door leaves back to the sage's chamber to the south.
name: Small Chamber

desc:
The tunnel seems to be twisting every short while, making it hard to keep track
on what direction you are moving to. Its appearance is clearly natural, although
the marks of occasional artificial widening can be seen here and there on the
walls and the ceiling.
name: A Twisting Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel bends to the north-west, having a steeply descending slope towards
the north. The floor is covered with some dust and debris and is apparently not
very often walked upon.
name: A Twisting Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel floor is steeply descending to the north here and quite a few
footholds have been hewn into the stone ground to offer some support. You notice
a few cracks in the otherwise smooth rock walls. The tunnel continues to the
south, where it can be seen rising slightly and taking a left turn.
name: A Descending Tunnel

desc:
Here the tunnel splits into the three separate branches. The western one seems
to lead into the small dry cave, while the main tunnel continues to the east and
south. Towards south it can be seen rising steeply.
name: A Tunnel Fork

desc:
You are in a small dry cave with a few shelves by the walls. Steep cracks run
over most of the floor and ceiling which only a few rough wooden beams support.
Dust and debris are everywhere and the whole place looks rather deserted. A
tunnel is to the east, enshrouded in darkness.
name: A Storeroom

desc:
The stone tunnel runs east-west here. The air is somehow damp and there are few
stalagmites hanging from the ceiling. Tunnel walls are glistening because of the
small drops of condensed water and the floor has a slight downward slope towards
the east. The sound of tinkling water can be heard from there.
name: A Stone Tunnel

desc:
A great underground lake stretches out in the cavern, its smooth surface is
black and undisturbed. Stalagmites can be seen in the ceiling and occasional
waterdrops break the silence with quiet plops when hitting the water surface.
The rising tunnel leaves to the west and neither light nor sound exist.
name: An Underground Lake

desc:
This is a spacious cavern with reddish stone walls. The air is somewhat warmer
here and loud snoring can be heard everywhere. Bundles of blankets and rugs are
lying all around, as well as the remnants of food. The only apparent exit from
this place is through a tunnel to the south.
name: Sleeping Quarters

desc:
Here the passage becomes slightly narrower and bends yet again, this time to the
north and east. It has a slight downward slope towards the north and the air
seems to get warmer there.
name: A Narrow Passage

desc:
The passage bends to the southwest, running deeper into the heart of the
Caradhras. Far away to the south, one might see something that looks like a
faint light. However, whether it is daylight or firelight or even a mirage
tricking the weary cannot be known.
name: A Turn in a Passage

desc:
A small cave leaves the main tunnel to the west here. The floor is covered with
debris and the walls are roughly hewn into the solid rock of the mountain. The
obvious intention has been to expand the main tunnel eastwards but for some
reason the work has stopped.
name: Small Cave

desc:
Here the small grotto leaves the passage running west of here. The ceiling and
walls are blackened by the soot. A few wooden benches are by the walls and a
cauldron of stew is bubbling on a low fire here. A mound of bones and guts is
towering in the corner.
name: A Dark Grotto

desc:
You have entered a high majestic cavehall. The torches on the walls are burning
with a yellow flame, emitting a dim light and smoke. The walls are of the red
stone of Caradhras and the soil below has been trampled hard by countless feet.
East of you, a tunnel leaves upwards into the mountain.
name: High Cavehall

desc:
The red rock walls of the cavehall disappear into the darkness high above. The
torches on the wall emit a faint yellow light. West of here, the tunnel
intersection can be seen in the eerie darkness. The cavehall continues to the
north and east.
name: High Cavehall

desc:
You are standing at the north-west corner of the dimly lit cavehall, which
continues to the east and south. The red rock walls, blackened by the smoke from
the torches beam high above your head, the ground has been worn perfectly even
by many feet. The faint sounds of yelling and growling seem to be coming from
the east.
name: High Cavehall

desc:
In its north-east corner, the cavehall seems to become considerably lower, the
faint outline of the ceiling can be guessed through the darkness. To the east
you notice a small niche and a passage with a slight downward slope leaves to
the north. Sounds of metal pounding against metal are coming from the east.
name: High Cavehall

desc:
Here the stairs have been carved into the rock, rising sharply upwards. On the
wall, a thick rope is running through the metal loops fastened to the stone
wall, offering some support. Here and there, the steps are rather worn out.
Occasional breezes of fresh air are coming from above and darkness seems to get
less dense in this direction.
name: A Winding Stone Staircase

desc:
This is a low room with grey rock walls, dimly lit by six narrow windows,
through which the icy wind is lashing in. Looking out of those you can see the
Misty Mountains stretching far and wide to all directions, with the Redhorn Pass
winding between the snow-capped peaks and dark crevices. To the north, Caradhras
is rising to the sky in its splendour. A stone stairway descends into the
darkness below.
name: The Redhorn Pass Watchtower

desc:
You are in an ancient tunnel that winds east. It has not been used in centuries.
It slopes downward at an alarming rate and you have trouble keeping your
balance.
name: Hidden Tunnel

desc:
You are in an ancient tunnel that goes south and west. It appears it has not
been used in centuries. The tunnel is very steep and you must be careful not to
fall.
name: Hidden Tunnel

desc:
You are an ancient tunnel that heads north. It does not seem to have been used
in ages. It is very steep and you had best be careful walking here.
name: Hidden Tunnel

desc:
You find yourself shivering in a dark, smelly cave. Strange sounds echo in the
darkness. Great wooden doors lie against the north and east walls.
name: Entrance of the Cave

desc:
The awful stench here is very unnerving. If that is not enough of a reason to
leave there are several trolls here. They are currently resting on what you
might call cots but they probably will not be resting for too long. You can see
that numerous bones lie on the floor, perhaps the remains of adventurers like
yourself.
name: Trolls Sleeping Cave

desc:
You can think of only one thing and that is how to rid your nose of the awful
smell. You eyes glance around the cave and you determine that this is the
sleeping cave for large, very cruel, and probably quite nasty mountain trolls.
The floor is littered with bones, trash, and broken weapons and armour.
name: Trolls Sleeping Cave

desc:
This room has served the trolls as both a prison and treasure room. Many dwarves
have sat in this very place awaiting dinner at the hands of the mountain trolls.
You notice 'treasures' lie upon the shelves. Bones, dead lizards and other trash
are heaped with care upon the racks.
name: Dark Cellar

desc:
Large, jagged boulders lay scattered on the cavern floor. The thick scent of
mildew and fungus is overwhelming. Light trickling of water reveals an icy pool
of water in the back of the cave. Eastwards, an arched entrance allows access
onto a stone shelf.
name: A Dank Grotto

desc:
Narrow stone walls prohibit much lateral movement in this icy pool. Various
pieces of jagged rock protrude on the sides of the chamber making it necessary
for all movement to be deliberate and cautious. Upwards, a faint shimmering of
light can be seen in contrast to the utter blackness in the pool.
name: Cavern Pool

desc:
The blackened stone walls and faint trickling of the underwater chamber to the
east emphasises the isolation of this small cavern. Eastwards, it is possible to
travel through a U-shaped tunnel into a deep pool, but it is a narrow passage
and requires time to maneuver. A big boned, decaying skeleton lies on the floor
in disarray.
name: A Blackened Cavern

desc:
You are in a room so low, that even smaller orcs cannot enter here unbent. The
acrid stench of urine and rotten meat fills your nostrils and makes your eyes
itch. The ground is soiled and wet, covered by a layer of animal hair and a few
hunks of half-eaten meat are lying around.
name: A Warg Pen

desc:
The ground falls away abruptly south of the pass. Here and there lies a scrawny
scrub-brush grasping the stony ground as the trail continues to the north and
east. Looking up the mountain eastward, snow gathers in the shadows and
depressions of the ground.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
The path travels north and west from here, where boulders of varying size dot
the area. They appear to have fallen from higher up the mountain, though just
how remains unclear. Where not covered by snow they have a distinct red hue.
When the weather permits, the air is crystal clear here on the side of this
greatest of the Misty Mountains.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
Oddly placed in the middle of the mountains, a small pile of stones has been
piled up here, but in a place where such rocks would not gather on their own.
Instead, this mound is in the midst of otherwise level and clear terrain.
Perhaps, long ago, some unlucky traveller was laid to rest here, when the
mountains proved too treacherous.
name: Among the Mountains

desc:
To the north the chill winds seem to increase dramatically, rolling down a steep
incline from the peaks above as if intent only on making travel that way even
harder. The wind gusts down rhythmically, sounding almost like the flap of giant
wings. Though a fairly gentle slope descends to the west of here, the eastern
and southern rock walls are impassable.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Like a massive finger of stone, this tall peak reaches high into the skies. All
around the snowy slopes fall away, save far to the east where great peaks rage
into the heavens. To the south a decline can be spotted worming its way down the
mountain slopes. The air is heavy with the sound of strange voices chanting,
whispering, cursing.
name: Howling Mountain

desc:
A channel originating to the southeast runs on down the mountain to the north.
Perhaps it was once the course of a stream, but it has long since run dry. Tall
peaks loom above to the east and south, their snowy heads reaching far up into
the clouds. The land slopes upward at a more modest grade to the west, along the
flanks of this mountain spur.
name: Lower Slopes of a Spur

desc:
Fractured rockfaces, lined with ice and permafrost, rise obstinately towards the
west, making travel in that direction impossible for even the most resolute
mountaineer. In all other directions, the rocky crags and mountainous terrain
become slightly less perilous at ground level, allowing for ingress and egress
if enough effort is applied. Above ground level, several boulders and columns of
rock hang precariously between gaps in the rock.
name: Jagged Mountainside

desc:
Jagged fingers of rock thrust up from thick, white blankets of crystalline snow.
Chunks of scree have fallen from high above, pocking the harsh landscape with
ashen craters of various depths. Sheer rock faces, marbled with ice-filled
cracks and chipping stone form these natural, towering edifices.
name: Steep Mountainside

desc:
Sharp fingers of snow-capped mountain peaks frame the western horizon like
massive, gnarled claws reaching up to tear the sky asunder. In all directions
these jagged peaks rise to unknown heights as they pierce the clouds that cling
desperately to the cold, dark stone high above. The only path down from this
isolated precipice is through a narrow cleft to the north.
name: Jagged Precipice

desc:
Hidden from view by the surrounding peaks, this trail can only be detected from
within or above. Although this place seems to be unknown by its careful
placement in the mountains, the path seems well worn, though the ground shows no
obvious sign of foot or hoof or wheel rut.
name: A Secret Mountain Path

desc:
Passing through the barren mountains and continuing south, this nature of this
road is a bit mysterious; perhaps it was created for easier travel between
certain places, for roads in this area are rare and out of the way. However, the
way in which it has been hidden from spying eyes may imply that its purpose is
not so simple.
name: A Secret Mountain Path

desc:
Bending to the west and north along the banks of a wide river, this path seems
to vanish from sight into the mountains to the north. Plains extend out of sight
to the west, seen beyond a few shorter mountain peaks; this river flows freely
from the east to the west.
name: A Bend in the Path

desc:
This area of the mountains seems devoid of life. cold dead rock lies all around.
A narrow path leading north and east has been stomped into the ground. High
thornbushes to the sides of the trail keep the path hidden from outside view.
Faint sounds of activity trails in from the south.
name: Secret Path

desc:
This area of the mountains is barren and lifeless. Away to the north the
mountains give way some low hills which are crossed by a small river. Running
east and west, a partially secluded trail makes its way through these steep and
rocky mountains.
name: Secret Path through the Mountains

desc:
The ground is hard and barren as the path leads north and west. Higher ground
rising steeply to the east and south serves as a detour for those wishing to
head into the mountains. Sounds of splashes seem to indicate the presence of
water nearby and the way to the west becomes more secluded and hidden by rising
ground.
name: Secret Path through the Mountains

desc:
Jagged and rocky hills stand all around, completely obstructing any passage to
the west. The ground is scattered with loose sharp rocks which could do
significant injury to the careless traveler. Many sharp crevices cast dark
shadows among the hills. The faint echo of splashing water reverberates from the
hills to the north and east while a quiet path leads southwards.
name: Rocky Mountains

desc:
The river winds its way around the hills as they rise up in its path. Pebbles
lining the riverbed make the ground hard and loose. One must carefully plant
each foot as the rushing frigid water makes it difficult to keep balanced. The
river flows from the east and heads north. To the west there seems to be a path
leading from the river.
name: Narrow River in the Mountains

desc:
You are standing among a range of hills that lie partly along the Misty
Mountains. A river flows from the high mountains to the east and heads towards
the Glanduin in the western horizon. The mountains stand tall and proud, their
snow-capped peaks defying the sky with their magnificence. Upstream to the east,
a parade of water tumbles towards you while the way to the south seems much
drier.
name: Narrow River in the Mountains

desc:
Among the high hills you wander, exploring further into the mountains. Compared
to the Misties, however, these hills are like children among adults. Ice covered
peaks dominate to the east, lending an awesome, if not daunting, view. A low
river has dug its bed through the hills and the only workable path seems to be
along the river.
name: Narrow River in the Mountains

desc:
A thin trail consisting of a few small patches of hard ground formed from
passing feet heads west. The surroundings are bare and the ground is flat. To
the southeast the great Misty Mountains rise up in horizon. Sounds of lapping
water emanate from the south while thick green vegation sprouts up from that
direction.
name: Secret Path in the Plain

desc:
At this point, you are moving close from the Hithaeglir proper, in a sea of low
bushes. To the south the Misty Mountains stretch high above you, their snowy
peaks pointing towards the heavens. The dangers, both natural and otherwise,
cause you to slow your step and view the beauty in a more cautious manner.
name: Bushy Plains

desc:
You are standing near the tall peaks of the Hithaeglir. All around you they
stand proudly. The air coming from the high altitude is cold and chill winds
that dart among the mountains like spirits of eternal winter. Tales of deeds
both daring and dark churn in your mind leaving you humbled against the grandeur
of the scene.
name: Bushy Plains

desc:
The hills here are quite high and anywhere else they would be considered
mountains. However, among these peaks, they are but smaller versions of the
incomparable Misty Mountains. From this height though, it appears possible to
move among the mountains themselves. Perhaps that would not be the wisest choice
but at heart you are still an adventurer.
name: The Hills between the Misty Mountains

desc:
Standing impossibly high, the Misty Mountains rise about you. Their defiance of
the laws of nature is a mute but unending testimony to the grandeur here. The
hills you stand on now are quite high, even enabling access to the mountains to
the north and south.
name: The Hills between the Misty Mountains

desc:
The ground here is semi-smooth albeit scattered with sharp rocks and fallen
scree. Once an active wash-out, it now serves as a mountainous pathway which
continues to the west and south; the latter of which is obscured with mist and
shadow. Walls of a dark stony colour loom high above to the east and north,
denying even the most skilled mountaineer passage.
name: Mountain Pass

desc:
The floor of this mountain valley is rather smooth and easy to traverse, the
product of long erosion by snow melt and runoff. The mountain slopes to the
north and south are likewise smooth, in this case presenting a total denial to
any attempts to traverse them.
name: Mountain Pass

desc:
The ground within this mountainous valley is fairly smooth, with indications
that it was once the path of least resistance for melting snow and water
run-off. To the south, however, the mountainsides loom overhead to dizzying
heights, and offer no purchase for hands or feet.
name: Mountain Pass

desc:
Sheer precipices loom high overhead to the north and south, seemingly closing in
towards each other high above where numerous boulders and crags can be seen. To
the east and west, the mountainous terrain remains parted in a natural valley
for a great distance. The ground, although rocky and uneven, allows a fairly
unobstructed path to continue onward.
name: Mountain Passage

desc:
Far above, towering snow-capped peaks loom ominously against the sky like
hideously nailed claws raking at the unfettered heavens. Whether by water
erosion or some other natural occurrence, a semi-smooth stretch of uneven rock
extends towards the east and west.
name: Sheer Mountainsides

desc:
Dark rock in the shape of towering, jagged finger-like formations reaches
upwards as if it were a clawed hand raking at the heavens. Although broken rock
and scree litter the ground here, passage is not impossible in all directions
save the south, where a fractured rock wall spreads far and wide.
name: Jagged Peaks

desc:
Massive rock formations, fractured, cracked, and failing, enclose the terrain
here save to the east, where between two great leaning pillars of stone passage
back out may be had. High above, nestled between a multitude of mountain peaks,
lie several boulders and large, broken pieces of rock. Occasionally, scree can
be heard in the distance tumbling down the steep stone walls, crashing into the
ground with blurred velocity.
name: Jagged Peaks

desc:
The ground within this mountainous valley is fairly smooth, with indications
that it was once the path of least resistance for melting snow and water
run-off. To the north, the mountains loom overhead to dizzying heights, their
tops shrouded in mist and cloud.
name: Mountain Peak

desc:
Jagged rock walls tower ominously high above, threatening to drop scree and
boulders down at any time. Perched precariously atop the stony shelves, several
large rocks are on the precipice of tipping into a downward descent. To the
west, as well as the south, the natural mountainous valley turns ever onward.
name: Sloping Mountainside

desc:
Cradled between two steep, jagged mountainsides to the north and south, a
natural depression runs east and west. High overhead, numerous boulders and
misshapen fingers of broken rock lay precariously wedged between crags of stone.
Far in the distance, this rocky ravine appears to curve northward to the west,
and southward to the east.
name: Mountainous Depression

desc:
Jagged outcroppings of dark, cold-cracked stone line the towering walls of
mountainous rock that thrust upwards to the northeast and southwest. To the
northwest and southeast, somewhat-even ground sprawls outward, evenly distanced
between the haphazard wall formations, as if each side of the path took great
care to be as far from the dense serration of rock that looms to either side.
name: Jagged Mountain Path

desc:
Twisting from the south, an uneven yet traversable mountain pass disappears into
the empty, dark depths of a cavernous void at the base of an enormous rockface.
One is able to see this cave slowly tapers inward for a few feet before all view
is swallowed up in darkness. Several enormous stalactites hang from the mouth of
the cave, the iron traces from the rock staining the tips an almost blood-red.
Beneath these, stalagmites have formed from the mineral deposits dripping from
the teeth-like formations overhead, staining the sides the same reddish colour.
From a distance, it resembles the blood-stained maw of a wereworm, rather than
the mouth of a cave.
name: Mouth of a Cavernous Passage

desc:
A narrow mountain trail is running along the slope of the Caradhras, winding
between the chasms and rocks and taking sharp turns every now and then. To the
east, the red rock of the Caradhras is rising steeply to the heights. The path
is surely not often walked upon, covered with debris and loose rocks, obviously
fallen from the higher mountains.
name: A Trail at the Slope

desc:
The winding trail running from the east takes a sharp turn around the high rock
and seems suddenly to stop just a short distance north of here. To the west you
can descend the mountain side, which does not seem to offer much safety but you
should still be climbable with caution.
name: A Turn in the Trail

desc:
The snow-capped peaks of the Misty Mountains surround you from all sides. Far at
the horizon to the west, the green lands of Hollin lie, barely visible at the
distance. Immediately to the east, a narrow path is winding between the rocks.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
The path running from the south seems to stop here. The wind is howling over the
deserted land and finding a safe foothold becomes increasingly difficult. Near
to the east, a wide ravine opens between the mountain top. Some low grass seems
to be growing there.
name: A Mountain Ridge

desc:
Mountain peaks rise majestically on all sides of this secluded ravine. A cool
breeze rustles the grasses on the ground. More mountains lie immediately to the
north and west while a dark cave opening can be seen to the east.
name: A Ravine

desc:
A narrow opening at your west extends to an ample natural cave. The air is
slightly warmer here than outside and a strong smell of animals permeates the
room. Now and then the howling wind lashes in from the cave mouth, but otherwise
it is pretty quiet. A few bones are lying all around on the cave floor.
name: A Dry Cave

desc:
The Misty Mountains stretch far and wide all around you. As you move up the
slope, the air becomes increasingly thin, making your breathing a laborous task.
To the west, the ground gets lower while a wide open ravine opens up in the
south. The Redhorn rises to the sky in the east.
name: Slopes of Caradhras

desc:
The mountains are rising sharply towards east here and, in a short distance,
seem too steep to climb. Cold and strong wind is making it hard to walk and you
have to concentrate on every step you take. Both north and south of you, the
mountains rise almost straight into the sky, to the east the Caradhras is
towering above the peaks of lesser mountains.
name: Rising Mountains

desc:
Mountains, more mountains as far as your eyes can see. The stone ground is bare,
without any vegetation. Rocks are lying loosely all around, ready to scatter
away as soon as your feet touch them. North of here, a deep chasm blocks the way
while to the west the rising ground is too steep to pass.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
The mountains slope slightly downward to the west. In this direction a majestic
view over the lower peaks of the Misties opens, and even away farther, the
rolling ground of Hollin can be seen. It might be a good idea to proceed with
caution to avoid the chasms hidden below the snow.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
You have travelled far south now. There is an old path here, covered by the
grass and the bushes. The grass has not been matted down in any particular area.
It appears that this area has few travelers.
name: Path in the Hills

desc:
High snow-capped peaks cover your view east. The air is cold, coming from the
Celebdil, and you should probably keep moving. If you take the mountain trail
you should meet bitter cold, if no worse, before you come down to the other
side. It will not help you to keep so secret that you are frozen to death. When
you leave here, where there are still a few trees and bushes, you should carry a
faggot of wood, as large as you can bear.
name: Path in the Hills

desc:
Soon your way becomes steep and difficult. The twisting and climbing road has in
many places almost disappeared, and is blocked with many fallen stones. A bitter
wind swirls among the rocks. Your mind starts to wander and you begin to hate
the cruel wind that seems intent upon chilling your body. It seems the mountains
themselves gaze upon you as you march, malevolent and cruel, wishing misfortune
for any creature that roams upon two feet.
name: Foot of the Redhorn Pass

desc:
Laboriously you climbed a sharp slope and halt for a moment at the top. Numerous
peaks rise high in the east. The peaks you can see are completely covered in ice
but the Celebdil, the highest one, is hidden in the clouds. The trail changes
here, while it starts to climb into the mountains. It looks like you will have
to return the way you came or try to cross the Redhorn Pass.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
You have reached the beginning of the RedHorn Pass. You hear eerie noises in the
darkness round you. It may be only a trick of the wind in the cracks and gullies
of the rocky wall, but the sounds are those of shrill cries, and wild howls of
laughter. Stones begin to fall from the mountain-side, whistling over your
heads, or crashing on the path beside you. Every now and again you hear a dull
rumble, as a great boulder rolls down from hidden heights above.
name: The Redhorn Pass

desc:
Your company now gather together as close to the cliff as you can. It faces
southwards, and near the bottom it leans out a little, so that you hope it will
give some protection from the northerly wind and from the falling stones. But
eddying blasts swirl round you from every side, and the snow flows down in ever
denser clouds.
name: Near a Cliff Shelter

desc:
The trail here is protected by a cliff-wall, the only shelter in the RedHorn
Pass. Below your refuge are white humps and domes and shapeless deeps beneath
which the path that you had trodden is altogether lost; but the heights above
are hidden in great clouds still heavy with the threat of snow.
name: Under a Cliff-wall

desc:
This den feels warm and comfortable to live in assuming you can imagine yourself
being a bear. The little opening to the south allows only a slight draft of the
cold mountain air into the cozy cave. Many bears have been born and trampled the
cave on frigid winter nights.
name: Bear Den

desc:
This tunnel haphazardly slants downward at an almost vertical angle. Uneven
stairs are roughly hewn into the rocky floor. In places, a partial collapse of
tunnel has covered the stairs in a layer of loose stones and dirt. The footing
here is slippery and any movement will be treacherous.
name: A Steeply Descending Passage

desc:
This shallow cave shows signs of recent animal habitation. A cracked humanoid
skull lies in one corner and various bones are scattered about. From the south
wall a narrow tunnel climbs steeply. To the east a jagged opening leads out to
the windy slopes of the misty mountains.
name: A Small Cave

desc:
The passage is running even deeper into the heart of the Redhorn northwards,
where it appears to soon bend to the left. To the south, a flickering torch can
be seen. Smoke eminates from the dark grotto to the east as well as the sound of
trickling water. The passage floor is worn out of regular use, with few
occasional sharper rocks still remaining here and there.
name: A Descending Passage
